chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – Divorce of the pregnant woman

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/140)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/140)
text: طَلَاقُ الْحَامِلِ وَاحِدَةٌ فَإِذَا وَضَعَتْ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا فَقَدْ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Abu Nasr, from Jameel, from Ismail Al Ju’fy,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Divorce of a pregnant woman is once. So when she places what is in her belly, so she has been irrevocably divorced from him’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn abu Nasr from Jamil from ’Isma‘il al-Ju‘fiy who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Divorce of a pregnant woman is one and when she gives birth she become stranger to him.’”
